chemicals.concentration_notes: These crystals are specifically concentrated in the sap of the plant.
chemicals.description: in cats: Upon ingestion or contact, these crystals cause dermal (skin) and oral mucosal irritation as well as edema (swelling). 4. Notes on
chemicals.name: Calcium oxalate crystals

chemicals.concentration_notes: These compounds are found in the leaves and seeds.
chemicals.description: in cats: Saponins are irritants that contribute to the plant's ability to cause irritation and swelling upon ingestion. 4. Notes on
chemicals.name: Saponins

chemicals.concentration_notes: These oils are located in the leaves and seeds.• Houseplants and Ornamentals Toxic to Animals - Toxicology - Merck Veterinary Manual
chemicals.description: in cats: These oils act as irritants to the skin and mucous membranes. 4. Notes on
chemicals.name: Acrid volatile oils

description: Century Plant (Agave americana) is a large, dramatic succulent commonly found in gardens and as a container plant. Despite its striking appearance, it is toxic to cats. The plant's sap contains irritating saponins and calcium oxalate crystals, and the leaves have extremely sharp terminal spines capable of causing physical puncture injuries. Contact with the sap causes immediate chemical burns and dermatitis; ingestion triggers gastrointestinal irritation.
name: Century Plant
safetyNotes: Symptoms: Drooling, vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain from ingestion; skin redness, blistering, and swelling from sap contact; puncture wounds from spines.
Sap is the primary chemical hazard: It causes both contact irritation and, if ingested, GI distress. The clear sap can squirt when a leaf is broken.
Action: Rinse skin or mouth thoroughly with water after any contact. Seek veterinary care if significant sap was ingested or skin reactions are severe.

symptoms.name: Oral Mucosal Irritation (pain and irritation of the mouth)
symptoms.notes: The sap contains calcium oxalate crystals which cause physical irritation to the lining of the mouth and throat upon ingestion.Edema (Swelling)
symptoms.onset: Immediate (Calcium oxalate crystals typically cause immediate pain upon chewing).

symptoms.name: Edema (Swelling)
symptoms.notes: Swelling can occur in the mouth or on the skin where the plant sap has come into contact with the animal.Dermal Irritation

symptoms.name: Dermal Irritation (Skin irritation/Dermatitis)
symptoms.notes: The sap of the Century Plant is an irritant. Contact with the skin can cause redness, irritation, and swelling.Note on Gastrointestinal Upset: While the sources specifically highlight irritation and edema for Agave americana, they also note the presence of saponins in the leaves and seeds1,2. In oth

treatments.description: The veterinarian will immediately assess the cat for signs of laryngeal edema (swelling of the throat and upper airway). If the swelling is severe enough to obstruct breathing, they may need to secure an airway (intubation) or provide oxygen support.
treatments.name: Airway Assessment and Stabilization
treatments.notes: The calcium oxalate crystals found in the sap can cause significant edema (swelling) of the oral mucosa and throat. While rare, if the swelling restricts airflow, this becomes a life-threatening emergency requiring immediate intervention before other treatments.

treatments.description: ◦ Oral: Thoroughly rinsing the cat's mouth with cool water to flush out the needle-like calcium oxalate crystals and remaining plant material. ◦ Dermal: If sap is on the fur or skin, the cat will be bathed with mild liquid dish soap and warm water to remove the irritant oils and sap.
treatments.name: Decontamination (Oral and Dermal)
treatments.notes: Agave sap is specifically noted to cause dermal and oral mucosal irritation. Removing the source is critical to stop the pain and prevent the cat from ingesting more toxins while grooming.

treatments.description: ◦ Medications: Administration of analgesics (pain relievers) to treat oral pain and anti-inflammatory medications to reduce swelling. Gastroprotectants or antiemetics may be given if vomiting occurs due to the saponins. ◦ Demulcents: Feeding small amounts of calcium-containing products (like milk or yogurt) may be suggested to bind the oxalate crystals and soothe the mouth (if the cat is not lacto
treatments.name: Symptomatic and Supportive Care (Pain Management & Fluids)
treatments.notes: Veterinary literature classifies the treatment for Agave americana specifically as "Symptomatic." The goal is to manage the immediate pain and irritation until the reaction subsides. The prognosis is generally good to excellent once the irritation is managed.
